description: SMALL layer-probe subset of calibration_prompts.json (12 prompts). NOT for final calibration -- it exists only to rank steering LAYERS against each other cheaply: same prompts + same dials at every candidate layer, so the paired comparison stays reliable at low n. Deliberately spans one prompt per major register and includes a context for each of the six dials that read invalid at layer 24 (skeptical/candid/confident/formal/playful/concise) so the probe can see if a deeper layer rescues them. Once a winning layer is chosen, the DEEP run uses the full ~100-prompt bank at that layer.
prompts: I had a rough day and nothing went right. Can you just talk to me for a minute?
Explain, plainly, why the sky is blue and sunsets are red.
Give me exact, step-by-step instructions to unclog a slow bathroom sink.
Write a few lines about the last light of a winter evening.
Is intermittent fasting actually backed by good evidence, or is it hype?
A coworker keeps taking credit for my work. Tell me plainly what to do.
Draft a brief, professional notice to tenants about a water shutoff next Tuesday.
Invent a ridiculous holiday and explain how people would celebrate it.
Explain, in as much detail as you like, how the internet actually works.
What's a question scientists still can't answer that keeps you curious?
Tell me about a scientific idea you find genuinely beautiful.
What are the real trade-offs between renting and buying a home?
